"Torah is a living conversation — not a lecture." — Dr. Judy Freier

About Dr. Judy

She makes Torah
make sense.

For more than three decades, Dr. Judy Freier has been helping people discover that the Torah isn't a relic — it's a remarkably practical guide to the questions we're all wrestling with right now.

Known affectionately as "The Torah Lady," she has a rare gift for translating ancient text into the language of everyday life: relationships, identity, purpose, loss, and joy. Her approach is warm, accessible, and completely free of jargon.

Whether she's teaching a seminary class, leading a Shabbat retreat, or chatting over coffee, Judy brings the same energy — curiosity, humor, and a genuine belief that these words have something urgent to say to each of us, personally.

30+ Years Teaching Published Author International Speaker Retreat Leader

Invite Dr. Judy to Speak

Published Work

The Book

View Full Screen

Now Available

Talking Torah for Today:
36 Life Lessons from the Book of Genesis

Dr. Judy Freier brings the ancient stories of Genesis to vivid life — showing how the struggles, choices, and wisdom of our ancestors speak directly to the challenges we face today. Each of the 36 lessons is warm, practical, and instantly applicable. This is Torah not as religious obligation, but as genuine guidance for living well.

Order on Amazon Request a signed copy →

"The Torah doesn't ask us to be perfect. It asks us to be present — to show up, wonder, argue, and keep returning."

Dr. Judy Freier · The Torah Lady

Teachings & Essays

From the
Weekly Portion

Read all teachings →

Parasha · Bereshit

Why "In the Beginning" Is Actually About Right Now

The opening word of the Torah is not about cosmology. It's an invitation to ask who you are when nothing is yet decided.

October 12, 2025 Read →

Parasha · Lech Lecha

What Abram Teaches Us About Leaving What Feels Safe

Go forth — not for God's benefit, but for yours. This week's portion holds a surprising amount of career advice.

October 26, 2025 Read →

Reflection

The Gift of Struggle: Wrestling with the Angel as Metaphor

Jacob doesn't win the fight. He limps away. And yet he walks forward with a new name. Some gifts only arrive broken.

November 9, 2025 Read →
